- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Pre-generation, generator-judged contradiction discovery.
2	
3	Candidate pairs come only from scoped evidence rows that share prompt-derived
4	content vocabulary or a reported measure.  The judge classifies the relationship
5	between the two supplied claims; only confident, comparable conflicts survive.
6	"""
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	import json
10	import re
11	from collections import Counter
12	from collections.abc import Callable, Mapping, Sequence
13	from typing import Any
14	
15	from src.polaris_graph.settings import resolve
16	
17	
18	_TRUE_VALUES = frozenset({"1", "true", "yes", "on"})
19	_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[^\W\d_][\w'-]+", re.UNICODE)
20	_UNIT_RE = re.compile(r"(?:%|[A-Za-z]+(?:/[A-Za-z]+)?)")
21	_NUMBER_WITH_UNIT_RE = re.compile(
22	    r"[-+]?\d+(?:[.,]\d+)?\s*(%|[A-Za-z]+(?:/[A-Za-z]+)?)",
23	)
24	_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
25	    "about", "after", "also", "among", "and", "are", "because", "before",
26	    "between", "but", "can", "could", "during", "for", "from", "had", "has",
27	    "have", "into", "its", "may", "more", "not", "over", "reported", "study",
28	    "than", "that", "the", "their", "these", "this", "those", "through", "under",
29	    "using", "was", "were", "which", "while", "with", "within", "would",
30	})
31	
32	
33	def contradiction_mining_enabled() -> bool:
34	    """Return the central default-off switch at call time."""
35	
36	    return (resolve("PG_CONTRADICTION_MINING") or "").strip().lower() in _TRUE_VALUES
37	
38	
39	def _row_text(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
40	    return " ".join(
41	        str(row.get(name) or "")
42	        for name in (
43	            "title", "source_title", "subject", "predicate", "measure", "metric",
44	            "endpoint", "population", "period", "statement", "direct_quote", "snippet",
45	        )
46	    ).strip()
47	
48	
49	def _row_tokens(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> frozenset[str]:
50	    return frozenset(
51	        token.casefold()
52	        for token in _TOKEN_RE.findall(_row_text(row))
53	        if len(token) > 2 and token.casefold() not in _STOPWORDS
54	    )
55	
56	
57	def _row_measures(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> frozenset[str]:
58	    declared = {
59	        str(row.get(name) or "").strip().casefold()
60	        for name in ("measure", "metric", "endpoint", "outcome")
61	        if str(row.get(name) or "").strip()
62	    }
63	    units = {
64	        match.group(1).casefold()
65	        for match in _NUMBER_WITH_UNIT_RE.finditer(_row_text(row))
66	        if _UNIT_RE.fullmatch(match.group(1))
67	    }
68	    return frozenset(declared | units)
69	
70	
71	def cluster_candidate_pairs(
72	    rows: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]],
73	) -> list[tuple[dict[str, Any], dict[str, Any]]]:
74	    """Return unique pairs sharing a discriminating term or reported measure.
75	
76	    Term frequency is computed from this evidence pool, so no domain vocabulary
77	    is embedded. Each row uses its least-frequent repeated terms as cluster anchors.
78	    """
79	
80	    copied = [dict(row) for row in rows]
81	    tokens = [_row_tokens(row) for row in copied]
82	    measures = [_row_measures(row) for row in copied]
83	    frequencies = Counter(token for row_tokens in tokens for token in row_tokens)
84	    anchors: list[frozenset[str]] = []
85	    for row_tokens in tokens:
86	        repeated = [token for token in row_tokens if frequencies[token] > 1]
87	        if not repeated:
88	            anchors.append(frozenset())
89	            continue
90	        minimum_frequency = min(frequencies[token] for token in repeated)
91	        anchors.append(frozenset(
92	            token for token in repeated if frequencies[token] == minimum_frequency
93	        ))
94	    row_count = len(copied)
95	    pairs: list[tuple[dict[str, Any], dict[str, Any]]] = []
96	    for left_index, left in enumerate(copied):
97	        for right_index in range(left_index + 1, row_count):
98	            shared_terms = anchors[left_index] & anchors[right_index]
99	            shared_measures = measures[left_index] & measures[right_index]
100	            if shared_terms or shared_measures:
101	                pairs.append((left, copied[right_index]))
102	    return pairs
103	
104	
105	def _judge_prompt(
106	    research_question: str,
107	    left: Mapping[str, Any],
108	    right: Mapping[str, Any],
109	) -> str:
110	    left_id = str(left.get("evidence_id") or left.get("source_url") or "row_a")
111	    right_id = str(right.get("evidence_id") or right.get("source_url") or "row_b")
112	    return f"""You are comparing two scoped evidence rows before a research report is written.
113	
114	Research question:
115	{research_question}
116	
117	Row A ({left_id}):
118	{_row_text(left)}
119	
120	Row B ({right_id}):
121	{_row_text(right)}
122	
123	Decide whether the rows make incompatible empirical claims about the SAME quantity or relationship.
124	Different populations, methods, periods, measures, endpoints, or observed-versus-modelled results
125	are not automatically conflicts. If the claims cannot be compared on the same basis, classify them
126	as non_comparable. If they can both be true, classify them as compatible.
127	
128	Return one JSON object only:
129	{{
130	  "classification": "conflict" | "compatible" | "non_comparable",
131	  "confident": true | false,
132	  "reason": "one sentence stating the exact disagreement or comparability boundary",
133	  "subject": "shared subject",
134	  "predicate": "shared quantity or relationship",
135	  "measure": "shared measure, or empty string"
136	}}"""
137	
138	
139	def _parse_object(raw: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
140	    text = str(raw or "").strip()
141	    if text.startswith("```"):
142	        text = re.sub(r"^```(?:json)?\s*", "", text, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
143	        text = re.sub(r"\s*```$", "", text)
144	    try:
145	        value = json.loads(text)
146	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
147	        match = re.search(r"\{.*\}", text, flags=re.DOTALL)
148	        if match is None:
149	            return {}
150	        try:
151	            value = json.loads(match.group(0))
152	        except json.JSONDecodeError:
153	            return {}
154	    return value if isinstance(value, dict) else {}
155	
156	
157	def find_contradictions(
158	    rows: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]],
159	    research_question: str,
160	    judge_fn: Callable[[str], str],
161	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
162	    """Judge candidate pairs and retain only confident comparable conflicts."""
163	
164	    conflicts: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
165	    for left, right in cluster_candidate_pairs(rows):
166	        verdict = _parse_object(judge_fn(_judge_prompt(research_question, left, right)))
167	        if verdict.get("classification") != "conflict" or verdict.get("confident") is not True:
168	            continue
169	        left_id = str(left.get("evidence_id") or left.get("source_url") or "")
170	        right_id = str(right.get("evidence_id") or right.get("source_url") or "")
171	        reason = " ".join(str(verdict.get("reason") or "").split())
172	        if not left_id or not right_id or not reason:
173	            continue
174	        conflicts.append({
175	            "evidence_ids": [left_id, right_id],
176	            "subject": str(verdict.get("subject") or "").strip(),
177	            "predicate": str(verdict.get("predicate") or "").strip(),
178	            "measure": str(verdict.get("measure") or "").strip(),
179	            "reason": reason,
180	            "comparison_status": "conflict",
181	            "confidence": "confirmed",
182	        })
183	    return conflicts
184
